description:
This project presents the design and implementation of a
  Software Quality Observatory Orchestrator, a system that
  automates the analysis and evaluation of research software
  repositories. The platform integrates tools from the
  research software ecosystem, including SOCA for repository
  discovery and metadata extraction and RSFC (Research
  Software Fitness Criteria) for assessing software quality
  indicators.


  The system is implemented through a containerized
  architecture using Docker, while workflow orchestration is
  managed with n8n, enabling reproducible and automated
  execution of the analysis pipeline. The orchestrator
  retrieves repositories, processes them through the
  evaluation tools, and collects the resulting quality
  metrics.


  The resulting framework provides a scalable and modular
  approach for evaluating large collections of research
  software, contributing to improved software quality
  assessment, reproducibility, and automation in research
  software engineering and open science.
name: Software-Quality-Observatory-Orchestrator
releaseNotes:
Initial release of the Software Quality Observatory Orchestrator.

This version provides a containerized architecture for orchestrating
software quality assessment workflows using SOCA and RSFC.

Main features:
- Automated repository retrieval
- Integration with RSFC for quality assessment
- Workflow orchestration using n8n
- Docker-based deployment
